title: Skillful Means: Balancing Doing Mind and Being Mind Note. The terms “doing mind,” “being mind,” and “nothing-to-do mind” were first used by Jon Kabat-Zinn in Full Catastrophe Living (1990, 2013). BEING

pages.text:
71
From DBT Skills Training Handouts and Worksheets, Second Edition by Marsha M. Linehan. Copyright 2015 by Marsha M. Linehan. Permission
to photocopy this handout is granted to purchasers of this book for personal use only (see copyright page for details).
Mindfulness Handout 9 (Mindfulness Worksheets 7–9)
Skillful Means:
Balancing Doing Mind and Being Mind
Note. The terms “doing mind,” “being mind,” and “nothing-to-do mind” were first used by Jon Kabat-Zinn in Full Catastrophe Living (1990, 2013).
BEING
MIND
DOING
MIND
WISE
MIND
Doing Mind Is: • Discriminating Mind • Ambitious Mind • Goal-Oriented
When in doing mind,
you view your thoughts
as facts about the world.
You are focused on
problem solving and
achieving goals. Being Mind Is:
• Curious Mind
• Nothing-to-Do Mind
• Present-Oriented
When in being mind, you
view your thoughts as
sensations of the mind.
You are focused on the
uniqueness of each
moment, letting go of
focusing on goals.
Wise Mind Is: • A balance of doing and being • The middle path
When in Wise Mind, you: Use skillful means. Let go of having to achieve goals—and throw your entire self into working toward these same goals. Enhance awareness while engaging in activities.

ourspace_kindness_pass.title: Skillful Means: Balancing Doing Mind and Being Mind Note. The terms “doing mind,” “being mind,” and “nothing-to-do mind” were first used by Jon Kabat-Zinn in Full Catastrophe Living (1990, 2013). BEING

main_doability_needs: bed-level or seated options by default
oxygen/breathing, pain, vertigo, fatigue, mobility, and recovery time respected
ADHD/autism supports: transitions, low-stimulation starts, tiny visible steps
partial completion and stopping before a crash count as success
no shame, no body blame, no pressure to push through pain or dysphoria
ADHD transition support before task demands
caregiver burnout recovery treated as care, not avoidance
protected rest, boundaries, decompression, sensory needs, and people-pleasing checks
no assumption that Jasper has unlimited caregiver capacity

household_rules: no hour block may hold more than two tasks/tools
skills may be used as one-breath or one-field versions
survival days load critical care first and skip optional worksheets
rest, boundaries, decompression, and asking for help count as care
skills are for support, not grades

universal_kindness_rules: This is a support tool, not a test.
One line, one checkbox, one breath, or one tiny step counts.
Skipping a skill because the body is unsafe, exhausted, dysregulated, or overloaded is allowed.
Do not stack this with more than one other task inside the same hour.
Survival-day version always wins over perfection.
william_accessibility_adaptations: Use bed-level, seated, voice-note, or caregiver-assisted versions when needed.
Avoid pain escalation, respiratory strain, unsafe transfers, and dysphoria-triggering language.
Break worksheets into one-field chunks; stopping before a crash is success.
jasper_accessibility_adaptations: Start with transition support before expecting focus.
Protect caregiver burnout recovery; rest and boundaries are valid completion.
Use body-doubling, timers, low-stimulation versions, and one-minute starts.
tiny_steps: Name one body sensation or one fact.
Choose one kind next step or stop for rest.

routine_integration.start_the_day_use: eligible as a rotating daily skill, never more than one DBT/ADHD skill in an hour block
routine_integration.recommended_block: after body check or before decompression
